name: Red Velvet
description:
'''Red Velvet''' ({{Korean|hangul=레드벨벳}}) is a South Korean [[girl group]] formed and managed by [[SM Entertainment]]. They originally debuted on August 1, 2014, with the single "[[Happiness (Red Velvet song)|Happiness]]" with the four-member line-up of [[Irene (singer)|Irene]], [[Seulgi]], [[Wendy (singer)|Wendy]], and [[Joy (singer)|Joy]]. A fifth member, [[Yeri (singer)|Yeri]], joined in March 2015, following the release of their first extended play, the chart-topping ''[[Ice Cream Cake (EP)|Ice Cream Cake]]''. Sonically, the music of Red Velvet reflects their group name: their predominantly-[[Pop music|pop]] "red" side experiments occasionally with [[Electronic music|electronic]] and [[funk]], while their "velvet" side focuses on '90s-influenced [[Rhythm and blues|R&B]] with elements of [[ballad]] and [[Hip hop music|hip hop]].

Red Velvet's Korean-language discography includes three studio albums, one reissue album, one compilation album, and thirteen extended plays—all of which have topped the South Korean [[Circle Album Chart]]. Their singles "[[Red Flavor]]" and "[[Power Up (song)|Power Up]]" were number one hits on the [[Gaon Digital Chart]], whilst "[[Dumb Dumb (Red Velvet song)|Dumb Dumb]]", "[[Russian Roulette (Red Velvet song)|Russian Roulette]]", "[[Rookie (Red Velvet song)|Rookie]]", "[[Peek-a-Boo (Red Velvet song)|Peek-a-Boo]]", "[[Bad Boy (Red Velvet song)|Bad Boy]]", "[[Psycho (Red Velvet song)|Psycho]]", and "[[Feel My Rhythm]]" all reached the top three. The group also ventured into the Japanese music scene with the extended plays ''[[Cookie Jar (EP)|#Cookie Jar]]'' (2018) and ''[[Sappy (EP)|Sappy]]'' (2019) as well as the studio album ''[[Bloom (Red Velvet album)|Bloom]]'' (2022).

Red Velvet have received numerous [[List of awards and nominations received by Red Velvet|accolades]], most notably the [[Golden Disc Awards|Golden Disc]] New Artist Award in 2015, the [[Mnet Asian Music Awards|Mnet Asian Music Award]] for Best Female Group in 2017, the [[Korean Music Awards|Korean Music Award]] for Best Pop Song in 2018, and the [[Asia Artist Awards|Asia Artist Award]] for Song of the Year in 2019.  Their wide domestic influence has been recognized by ''[[Forbes Korea Power Celebrity|Forbes]]'' [[Forbes Korea Power Celebrity|Korea Power Celebrity]] (placing 11th in 2018 and fifth in 2019); internationally, they were named one of the most popular [[K-pop]] groups by ''[[Time (magazine)|Time]]'' and ''[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]]'', and the fifth most streamed K-pop artist worldwide on [[Spotify]] in February 2020.
